name: Apfelpfannkuchen (German Apple Pancakes)
recipeIngredient: 2 large cooking apples (Yellow Delicious or Granny Smith)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 475°F.
2. Peel, core, and thinly slice the apples. You should have approximately 1-1/2 cups of slices.
3. Melt 3 tbsp butter over medium-low heat in a small frying pan. Saute the apples until they are just tender. Keep apples warm while preparing the batter.
4. Place a 9 or 10-inch cast-iron skillet in the oven to heat for at least 5 minutes. The pan must be very hot for this recipe to work. When it is well heated, add the remaining 2 tbsp butter and put the skillet back in the oven. The butter should be very hot but not brown when you add the apples and batter.
5. In a blender, mix together flour, milk, vanilla extract, salt, and nutmeg until smooth.
6. Remove the skillet from the oven. Quickly arrange the warm apple slices over the melted butter. Pour the batter evenly over all the ingredients.
7. Bake for 15 minutes at 475°F, then reduce heat to 375°F and bake for an additional 10 minutes. The pancake will puff up and climb along the sides of the pan.
8. Sprinkle with confectioners sugar, then cut into wedges and serve with maple syrup.
Note: If you do not use apples, add 1/4 cup (1/2 stick) butter to the hot skillet.
